behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function/object seam inside the scoring model (threshold lookup + score calculation). The failure is incorrect rule logic for one transaction/category, which is reproducible with in-memory config and inputs. Rejection gate: no behavior text requiring OS/network/subprocess or external model judgment; text is deterministic rule application ('configured amount threshold for its merchant category'), so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test scoring function with table-driven merchant-category thresholds and boundary amounts (below/equal/above). Assert exact numeric score contribution when threshold is exceeded and catch misapplied category mapping or comparator bugs.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: model-judgment seam. Correctness depends on whether the real risk model recognizes a fraud pattern beyond explicit rules, which is output-quality dependent and potentially non-deterministic. Mock-hide: deterministic stubs would always 'pass' and cannot reveal degradation in real fraud-pattern detection. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one model judgment step, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation test against a labeled scenario set of cross-geo rapid micro-purchase patterns where rule thresholds stay below limits; call the real risk model and assert fraud probability/label meets acceptance criteria over repeated runs (or statistical threshold) to catch model drift/misclassification.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: local function seam for time-window counting logic per user. The described failure is counting/window-boundary logic, testable with synthetic timestamps and user IDs in memory. Rejection gate: behavior text does not require real clock, DB, network, or subprocess; therefore System is disqualified. It also does not depend on AI/ML output; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for velocity counter using fixed timestamps around window edges (inclusive/exclusive boundaries), mixed users, and out-of-order inputs; assert per-user counts exactly match configured window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam where scoring and risk outputs are combined into hold/review decision. Real failure appears when modules hand off flags and decision logic composes them incorrectly (OR vs AND). Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required (so not System), no dependence on real model quality (boolean flags can be controlled deterministically, so not Agentic), and no multi-step agent pipeline (not Workflow). Rejection gate for Unit: behavior explicitly concerns interaction of two model outputs ('flagged by risk model but not scoring model').
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring scoring-output adapter + risk-output adapter + decision service; feed case (scoring=false, risk=true) and assert transaction status=held_for_review. Include complementary truth-table cases to catch accidental AND logic or dropped flag propagation.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam for response composition/contract from two model result producers into reviewer-facing payload. Real failure is often schema/field-mapping loss across module boundaries (missing score/probability/flag source). Integration-brake: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess (System disqualified), no requirement for evaluating model judgment quality (Agentic disqualified), and no multi-step agentic chain (Workflow disqualified). Rejection gate for Unit: behavior text requires 'combined output' from both models, implying cross-module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test over the composition layer that receives scoring result and risk result, then asserts output contract contains numeric score, fraud probability, and per-model flag attribution fields used by review UI/workflow.
